name.description: PascalCase ASCII. Becomes the generated Rust struct name.

table.description: SQL table name. Consumed by the migration generator.

fields.description: Ordered map of `column_name` → field definition. Source order is preserved so the generated struct reads the same way as the JSON.

primary_key.description: SQL primary-key flag. The migration generator emits `PRIMARY KEY` on every field that carries this. Multiple fields with `primary_key` yield a composite key in the order they appear.

unique.description: Field-level shorthand for a single-column unique index. Equivalent to adding `{ "fields": ["<col>"], "unique": true }` to `indexes`.

default.description: SQL default expression embedded verbatim into the migration. Example: `"gen_random_uuid()"`, `"now()"`. No escaping is performed.

max_length.description: `VARCHAR(N)` length hint. Ignored for non-string columns.

references.description: Field-level shorthand for a foreign key. Equivalent to adding a `{ "field": "<col>", "references": "<Model>.<field>" }` entry to `foreign_keys`. Accepts the legacy object form too.

validate.description: Optional CEL validator. Syntactically validated at parse time; the runtime evaluator (returning 422 on `false` with the offending field name) lands once input parsing is implemented.

FieldType.description: Logical column types supported in `models/*.json`. Mapped to concrete Rust types in `codegen::model_field_type`.

FieldReference.description: The two accepted shapes of field-level `references`: - the structured form `{ "model": "Author", "field": "id", "on_delete": "..." }` - the dotted shorthand `"Author.id"`

OnDelete.description: `ON DELETE` actions accepted in declared foreign keys.

fields.description: Columns covered by this index, in order. Must be non-empty.

name.description: Optional explicit index name. When omitted, the migration generator derives one from the table + columns (e.g. `posts_slug_idx`).

references.description: `<Model>.<field>` reference. Resolved against the loaded model set; unknown references fail at load time.

on_delete.description: SQL `ON DELETE` action. Defaults to `restrict` to surprise no one.

name.description: Optional constraint name. Helps the migration emit `CONSTRAINT <name> CHECK (...)`, which is easier to alter or drop later.

expr.description: SQL expression embedded verbatim. The current parser does not validate it; the database rejects bad expressions at migration apply time.
